mnemonics.title: Green IT Pillars
mnemonics.content: The 3 Pillars of Green IT can be remembered as 'DRG': Design (green hardware), Reduce (energy usage), and Green disposal (e-waste management).

mnemonics.title: Energy Efficiency Steps
mnemonics.content: Steps to Energy Efficiency: 'V-S-C-M' - Virtualize, Consolidate, Monitor, and Manage power.

mnemonics.title: Data Center Cooling
mnemonics.content: Remember hot aisle/cold aisle containment: 'Hot rises, cold sinks - separate their ranks' to optimize cooling efficiency.

acronyms.usage: Metric to measure data center energy efficiency; lower is better, ideal is close to 1.0

acronyms.expansion: Green Recognition and Environmental Efforts in IT
acronyms.usage: A comprehensive approach to sustainable computing practices
Discarded electronics requiring special disposal due to hazardous materials
Includes purchase, operation, and disposal costs including environmental factors
Used to evaluate financial benefits of Green IT initiatives
Centralized desktop management that reduces energy and hardware needs

visualTips.visualization: Draw a circle representing total facility energy, with a smaller inner circle for IT energy. PUE = Big Circle ÷ Inner Circle. The more they overlap, the closer to 1.0 (efficient).
Picture one large table (physical server) with multiple plates (virtual machines) instead of having separate tables for each. Same resources, more utility.
Imagine rows of server racks like parking spaces - hot cars face hot cars, cold cars face cold cars. This creates efficient airflow patterns for cooling.
Draw a lifecycle: Manufacturing → Usage → Disposal → Recycling. Green IT aims to make the Disposal and Recycling circles smaller through longevity.

analogies.analogy: Like a city that never sleeps - data centers run 24/7, consuming power like streetlights and essential services, making efficiency crucial.
Like a carpool lane - instead of each person driving separate cars (servers), multiple passengers (virtual machines) share one vehicle (physical server), reducing traffic (energy).
Like switching from incandescent to LED bulbs - the initial investment is higher but long-term savings and efficiency make it worthwhile.
Like a fuel efficiency rating in cars - just as MPG shows how efficiently fuel is used, PUE shows how efficiently energy reaches the actual computing.
